---
title: "Trump Tweets Sentiment Analysis"
author: "Daniel Wang"
date: "2026-02-28"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(vader)

# Load data
df <- read.csv("filtered_trump_dat.csv")

# ── Run VADER on every tweet ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# vader_df() accepts a character vector and returns a dataframe with scores
vader_results <- vader_df(df$text)

# ── Combine with original data ────────────────────────────────────────────────
df_sentiment <- bind_cols(df, vader_results) %>%
  rename(
    vader_positive  = pos,
    vader_negative  = neg,
    vader_neutral   = neu,
    vader_compound  = compound   # main score: -1 (most negative) to +1 (most positive)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    sentiment_label = case_when(
      vader_compound >= 0.05  ~ "positive",
      vader_compound <= -0.05 ~ "negative",
      TRUE                    ~ "neutral"
    )
  )

# ── Quick summary ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("Sentiment label distribution:\n")
print(table(df_sentiment$sentiment_label))

cat("\nAverage compound score:", round(mean(df_sentiment$vader_compound, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")

# ── Plot sentiment over time ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
df_sentiment %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(date)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = date, y = vader_compound)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = sentiment_label), alpha = 0.2, size = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, color = "black", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("positive" = "steelblue",
                                "negative" = "tomato",
                                "neutral"  = "gray60")) +
  labs(title    = "Tweet Sentiment Over Time (VADER)",
       subtitle = "Compound score: -1 (most negative) to +1 (most positive)",
       x        = "Date",
       y        = "VADER Compound Score",
       color    = "Sentiment") +
  theme_minimal()

head(df_sentiment)
# ── Save results ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# write.csv(df_sentiment, "tweet_sentiment_vader.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
df_sentiment <- df_sentiment %>%
  select(-text...16) %>%
  rename(text = text...5)
```


```{r}
head(df_sentiment)
head(df)
```

```{r}
write.csv(df_sentiment, "trump_tweets_with_sentiment.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
